tickers.one_line_thesis: DataSection is betting the Neo Cloud franchise on hyperscaler-linked AI data centres in Japan (Inzai) and Australia, with a FY2026 revenue step-up to ¥33.6B still guided post-Apr-2026 delays; the May 2026 shift to NVIDIA B300 via Compal (~USD 325M) and a disclosed financing bridge improves execution visibility but raises contract-renegotiation, debt-timing, and Giga-order overhang risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary: DataSection is attempting a step-change from small legacy revenue to hyperscaler-scale AI data-center operations in FY2026. Management has named capacity, capex, and a financing bridge in English IR filings (inventory tier; extract failed this build), but durable competitive advantage is unproven and asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) fails on concentrated capex and customer concentration. At ¥4,490 (Stooq, 2026-05-25), base expected annual return (Lawrence) is 5.7% on normalized ¥90/share owner earnings (FY2026 guided EPS discounted for ramp risk), with bear -7.1% if delays persist. Upside ...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Japan's dominant exchange and clearing infrastructure — a Stahl croupier with ~58% operating margins and volume leverage — but earnings are cyclical with market turnover; do not treat FY2025 peak activity as a permanent baseline.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Japan Exchange Group is Japan's exchange and clearing toll booth with ~58% operating margins and stable moat characteristics. FY2025 was a volume spike year: revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5%), net income ¥79.1B, ROE 23.1%. We normalize owner earnings to ¥70/share, below peak EPS ¥76.81, to adjust for clearing pass-through and cyclical turnover. At ¥2,000.5 (2026-05-25), Lawrence base case implies 4.7% annualized return over ten years, well below the 15% hold band. Bull case ~7.9% if engagement persists; bear ~-2.1% on flat earnings. Filing evidence build confirms 399 inventoried documents; the lates...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Dominant e-commerce + AWS platform compounding at scale (FY2025 $717B sales, $78B NI) with AWS re-accelerating (+28% Q1-26) and high-margin ads/chips engines emerging — but AI capex has temporarily collapsed FCF, requiring disciplined valuation and capex ROI monitoring.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Amazon runs four reinforcing engines: retail logistics density, AWS, high-margin advertising (>$70B TTM), and custom silicon (Trainium/Graviton/Nitro). Q1 2026 consolidated operating income $23.9B (+30%) and AWS re-acceleration (+28%) confirm operating momentum. [Fact] TTM operating cash flow is $148.5B, but TTM FCF is only $1.2B because capex consumed nearly all OCF. [Inference] Q1 net income $30.3B includes ~$16.8B pre-tax gain on Anthropic and must be normalized for earnings-power analysis.

At ~$263 (Stooq, 2026-05-25), Lawrence base case uses $5.35/sh normalized FCF (OCF less mid-cycle...

tickers.one_line_thesis: AI data centre developer with accelerating revenue ($126.6M FQ3, +139% YoY) and 600+ MW of signed leases (CoreWeave, hyperscaler). FQ3 GAAP net income $100.9M includes $59.7M discontinued-operation gain (Cloud held-for-sale); continuing ops still loss-making on adjusted basis. Customer concentration and layered debt/preferred stack require capital-structure diligence before conviction sizing.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Applied Digital is transitioning from crypto-era hosting to a 1.2 GW contracted lease book across four AI Factory campuses. FQ3 2026 adjusted EBITDA was $44.1 million; GAAP net income was $100.9M but includes a $59.7M discontinued-operation gain on Cloud Services held-for-sale, so continuing economics still require adjusted metrics. The May 20, 2026 Polaris Forge 3 deal adds 300 MW and ~$7.5 billion base revenue with the same hyperscaler as Delta Forge 1, lifting disclosed contracted revenue to ~$31 billion. At $46.85 (Stooq, May 25, 2026), Marvin floor IRR is 11.5% on $1.25/sh forward owne...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global alternative-asset compounder earning durable fee streams on $614B fee-bearing capital with scaling insurance/wealth platform ($180B assets), active buybacks, and corporate simplification (BN+BNT merge) — DE-focused valuation, not GAAP NI.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Brookfield compounds fee-bearing capital ($614B, Q1 2026) with a scaling insurance/wealth pivot ($180B assets) and active capital return (buybacks >$1B BN/BAM YTD 2026). FRE grew 11% YoY; the firm raised $67B and deployed $53B in the quarter per the shareholder letter. At ~$45.93 (Stooq, 2026-05-25), Lawrence base 10yr IRR is 12.1% on LTM DE $2.54/sh (bear 8.4%, bull 14.7%), slightly below the 15% hold band. Stable moat from scale and relationships; partial dhando. Marvin proposes watch on math; prior hold is a human override for the allocator sleeve. Primary risk: fundraising drought compr...

tickers.one_line_thesis: CMSG is a cash-rich OTCQX bitcoin/scrypt miner where marked-to-market digital assets dominate GAAP earnings; the equity is largely a BTC-treasury and hash-share option under Horizon-affiliated governance — watch at 8.7% blended IRR (Marvin floor 7.7%); approved Substacks (LCI seigniorage) add partial treasury credit; fee redesign post-2026 and network-share trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: CMSG is an optionality name: loss-making mining ops plus a cash-rich treasury and Horizon-related governance. Normalized owner earnings (~$1.35/sh) blend interest income and breakeven mining, excluding crypto marks. At ~$30 (OTCQX, ~May 26, 2026), base expected annual return at today's price (Lawrence) is 7.7%, below the 15% bar; bull 18.8% requires BTC/treasury re-rating. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is unproven; asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial via NAV floor. We propose watch and size for OTC liquidity; reconcile with any FRMO/Horizon sleeve overlap.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global salvage-vehicle auction toll booth de-rated to ~$33 — oligopoly economics intact; base IRR 9.2% (core override) while monitoring U.S. assignment/fee growth and Q3 FY2026 trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Copart is a global salvage-auction oligopoly with fortress balance sheet, yard network scale, and float economics. Q3 FY2026 showed $1.24B revenue (+2.1%) with international strength and ASP +4.6% offsetting US unit decline. At ~$33.07, Lawrence base case implies ~9.2% annualized return over ten years on $1.25 mid-cycle FCF per share, below the 15% accumulate bar but above bear case 2.7%. Bull ~13.9% if volume re-accelerates and multiple expands toward historical premium. Asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is full: net-cash-like balance sheet and high ROIC core bound downside. We propose wat...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Wide-moat CRE platform de-rated to ~$33 (−54% YoY); Homes.com/Matterport distort GAAP — watch at 8.6% base IRR until normalized FCF proves out.

deep_dive.executive_summary: CoStar combines wide-moat CRE subscriptions with a contested residential land-grab. Normalized free cash flow per share is thin after Homes.com and acquisition amortization, but adj. EBITDA run-rate (~$644M annualized from Q1 2026) shows operating earnings power. At ~$33.21 (NASDAQ, ~May 26, 2026), base expected annual return at today's price (Lawrence) is 8.6% on $0.95 normalized FCF/share, below the 15% bar. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable in CRE; asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial with residential burn tail risk. We propose watch until Homes.com spend mode...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Decentralized VMS acquirer reinvesting FCFA2S at hurdle rates — base 16.0% IRR supports core stance; GAAP net income secondary to FCFA2S compounding.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Constellation is the reference serial acquirer of VMS: decentralized business units, Mark Leonard culture, and 100% of FCFA2S reinvested at disciplined hurdles. Filing extracts confirm Q1 2026 acceleration ($3.18B revenue, +20%) and FY2025 scale ($11.62B, total assets $16.17B, +26% YoY). At ~$1,933 USD/sh (CSU.TO C$2,668 converted), Lawrence base 10yr IRR is 16.0% (bear 10.6%, bull 20.0%), clearing the 15% hold band. Stable moat from hundreds of micro-franchises; partial dhando (premium multiple, M&A price inflation). Marvin proposes hold on math; approved stance core remains the incumbent ...

tickers.one_line_thesis: DBS-driven life science/diagnostics platform — hold at 10.1% base IRR (quality override); cyclically tied to biopharma funding and China diagnostics recovery.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Danaher is a DBS-driven compounder in biotechnology, life sciences, and diagnostics (post-Veralto separation). FY2025 FCF per share is about $7.34 (OCF $6.42B less capex $1.16B). Q1 2026 shows modest re-acceleration ($5.95B revenue, operating margin ~22.5%). At ~$172 (NYSE, May 2026), Lawrence base 10yr IRR is 10.1% (bear 5.8%, bull 14.2%), below the 15% hold band. Stable moat on validated workflows and consumables; partial dhando (cyclical end markets, premium multiple). Marvin proposes watch on math; approved stance hold remains a quality incumbent override. Primary risk: multi-year bioph...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Horizon Kinetics–affiliated holding company with concentrated stakes in exchange infrastructure (MIH) and HKHC — a look-through bet on Murray Stahl's ecosystem, not an operating croupier; value = sum-of-parts vs fair-value accounting noise.

deep_dive.executive_summary: FRMO is a permanent-capital vehicle tied to Murray Stahl's Horizon ecosystem. Book value per share is about $8.55; the OTC quote near $6.70 (Stooq, May 22, 2026) is roughly 22% below book with negligible debt and high insider ownership. Reported earnings are noisy because unrealized investment gains drove Q3 FY2026 net income to FRMO of $83.4 million; that is not operating cash flow. A ten-year free-cash-flow model is the wrong primary tool here. Our base case uses a five-year sum-of-parts catalyst path: payoff $18 per share from $6.70 implies about 21.9% annualized return, with a bear case...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Search/YouTube ads cash engine plus Google Cloud AI infrastructure (+63% Q1 2026) — wide-moat compounder betting massive capex ($180–190B 2026) on Cloud ROI while navigating AI disruption to Search monetization.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Alphabet is a dual-engine compounder: Google Services funds AI and Cloud investment while Cloud re-accelerates (+63% Q1 2026). Operating margin expanded to 36.1% in Q1 2026 (from 33.9% prior year). [Fact] GAAP diluted EPS $5.11 in Q1 includes large unrealized equity gains (~$37.7B); normalize before peer comparison. Cloud backlog and query volume at an "all-time high" (CEO, Q1 2026) support the widening moat narrative on infrastructure; Search faces long-run AI interface risk.

At ~$386 (NASDAQ, May 2026), Lawrence base 10yr IRR is 2.1% on reported $5.85 FCF/sh (bear -4.8%, bull 5.4%). A me...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global exchange and market-infrastructure croupier with 20 consecutive record revenue years and ~80% exchange margins — quality toll booth, but Lawrence base IRR ~11% at $156 sits below the 15% hold band on mid-cycle FCF; watch for pullback or bull-case volume.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Intercontinental Exchange is a toll collector on global market activity: exchanges, clearing, data, and mortgage rails with minimal principal risk. FY2025 filings confirm $9,931M net revenues, $4,929M operating income, and $28,915M stockholders' equity; Q1 2026 accelerated to $2,977M net revenues and $1,413M net income on strong Exchanges volume. At $153, Lawrence base case implies ~11.0% annualized return over ten years on $7.41 mid-cycle FCF per share, below the 15% hold band. A separate Hohn EPS bridge still implies higher near-term returns if volume momentum persists, but that reconcile...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Michigan mineral royalty optionality (>1M acres, Copperwood/Highland Copper leases) with patient balance sheet — asymmetric upside if mining develops, steady burn and single-tenant concentration until royalties activate.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Keweenaw Land is a Michigan mineral estate with a Copperwood free option on top of modest lease income. FY2024 book value is ~$12.70 per share; burn is ~$160K per year against cash and treasuries. At $55, price sits ~4.3x book, so floor_pass fails and dhando weakens versus the June 2024 $27 repurchase. Optionality overlay: Copperwood option yield ~12.4% (bull) at $4/lb Cu; Lawrence yield-curve base is -8.3% (lease income only). Bear path -17.8% toward book if Copperwood delays. We propose watch: asset math remains interesting but entry no longer clears the 15% bar.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Mesabi Trust is a contractual iron ore pellet royalty on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore (Silver Bay, MN): cash is cyclical commodity + bonus-tier optionality, with ongoing AAA arbitration (commenced Sep 2025) layered on statutory trust simplicity—hold at 8.4% normalized yield for the incumbent royalty sleeve; do not downgrade on depressed Q1 bonus alone.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Mesabi Trust is a contractual pellet royalty on Cliffs' Northshore mine with cyclical commodity exposure and a legal recovery option. FY2026 10-K confirms AAA arbitration commenced September 26, 2025 on idling and intercompany pricing; Horizon Kinetics holds 22.9%. Q1 2026 bonus royalty was zero despite tonnage doubling YoY; Q4 2025 still paid $1.04 million bonus, showing mechanics intact. Nine-month royalty income fell to $13.16 million on lower pricing and a February 2025 maintenance shutdown. At $25.68 (Stooq, May 26, 2026), normalized distribution yield is 8.4% (~$2.16/unit per year); e...

tickers.one_line_thesis: U.S. OTC market infrastructure and ATS operator — a Stahl croupier with subscription-heavy fees and expanding disclosure tiers (OTCID) and extended-hours trading — earnings scale with issuer count and activity, not balance-sheet risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary: OTC Markets is U.S. OTC listing and ATS infrastructure: subscription-heavy fees on disclosure tiers (including new OTCID tier) and extended-hours trading. Full-tier FY2025 and Q1 2026 filings confirm acceleration: net revenues +12% FY25 and +15% in Q1, with transaction revenue +38% on higher OTC Link activity. FY2025 mix was 81% subscription / 19% transaction; Q1 2026 shifted to 78% / 22% as volume picked up. OTCID contributed positive revenue in Q1. At ~$52, Lawrence base case implies 9.7% annualized return over ten years on ~$2.35 mid-cycle owner cash per share, below the 15% hold band. B...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Post-merger diagnostics platform (Quidel + Ortho) with improving adjusted margins but declining revenue — integration and non-respiratory growth must prove out before this is a compounder again.

deep_dive.executive_summary: QuidelOrtho is a diagnostics turnaround. Margins improved to 21.9% while revenue still falls; FY2026 guide is flat sales. Marvin floor: 10.9% IRR on $1.05/sh haircut owner cash. McIntyre external: ~$4 FCF/sh 2028, ~$60 (2.5yr frame, put-hedged). Blended best estimate: ~18% IRR on $1.45/sh, 10× exit, 55% / 45% weights (filings floor vs partial core normalization). Moat unproven; dhando partial. Proposed hold (middle between Marvin watch and external max-long; size for sleeve, not 20% fund weight).

tickers.one_line_thesis: Passive San Juan Basin gas NPI royalty trust — equity yield curve / time arbitrage on temporary Hilcorp capex deficit (no distributions since May 2024); re-rating potential when distributions resume if unit price discounts recoverable NPI rather than terminal wind-down.

deep_dive.executive_summary: San Juan Basin Royalty Trust is a passive gas NPI royalty with zero distributions since May 2024 while Hilcorp recovers excess production costs. FY2025 10-K confirms zero royalty income and no unit-holder distributions; Q1 2026 10-Q shows net deficit $4.64 million after $1.69 million net recovery in the quarter. Cash is $14,380; line of credit $750,514; going-concern doubt is disclosed. At ~$4.15 (Yahoo, May 26, 2026), base equity-yield-curve return is 5.8% over eight years ($6.50 payoff); bull 19.4% in six years if gas spikes and costs clear fast. Dhando is none today: zero cash flow, depl...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Essential financial intelligence — ratings, indices, and data subscriptions — a Stahl croupier / index infrastructure compounder with extraordinary margins; earnings leverage to issuance and AUM with cyclicality at the margin.

deep_dive.executive_summary: S&P Global is index and ratings infrastructure: fees on debt issuance, benchmark tracking, and data subscriptions with extraordinary margins. FY2025 filings confirm $15,336M revenue, $6,478M operating income, $4,471M net income, and $31,127M stockholders' equity; Q1 2026 revenue $4,171M (+10% YoY) with operating income $2,002M (+27% YoY) and diluted EPS $4.69 (+33% YoY). Mobility spin mid-2026 should simplify the portfolio toward core toll booths. At ~$411, Lawrence base case implies ~10.1% annualized return over ten years on ~$17.5 mid-cycle FCF per share, below the 15% hold band. Bear cas...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Swedish industrial serial acquirer of niche subsidiaries — decentralized Pabrai-style model with 2025–2026 margin/FCF turnaround underway; organic sales still negative while unprofitable units are pruned.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Teqnion is a proven decentralized serial acquirer with a strengthening margin story: FY2025 net sales +15% (nine acquisitions), EBITA +36%, organic -5% from deliberate exits, and equity ratio 42.8%. Goodwill impairment 73 MSEK (Q3 2025) flags past overpayment risk. At ~158 SEK, base expected annual return (Lawrence) is 13.7% on 10.08 SEK FY2025 FCF/share, below the 15% accumulate band. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable at the portfolio level; asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial with M&A risk. We propose watch unless price falls toward ~130 SEK for a ~15% base IRR.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong Adj. EBITDA growth ($254M FY2025) and ~2,500 miles of pipe — asset-heavy midstream toll model, not Stahl croupier; thesis blocked by IPO/combination complexity and Five Point sponsor control.

deep_dive.executive_summary: WaterBridge is Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong post-combination operating proof: Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA $102.9 million on 2,460 MBbl/d volumes. FY2025 adjusted EBITDA was $254 million, but GAAP losses, $1.47 billion debt, and Five Point sponsor control complicate economic attribution for Class A holders. At ~$31 (NYSE, May 22, 2026), base Lawrence 10-year IRR is 12.1% on maintenance-adjusted FCF per share of $2.13 (6%/4% growth, 14x exit). Cap-rate cross-check: ~8.9% EBITDA yield on ~$2.9B EV looks fairly valued for merchant midstream. Moat is unproven and dhando p...
